title: Build scaffold-app-from-web orchestrator — Phases F+G + the top-level binding verb
description: Top-level orchestrator CLI binding all 7 phases of scaffold-app-from-web. Invocation: `scaffold-app-from-web <url> [--name <slug>] [--kind demoGym|prototype|...]`. Phase A and B in parallel; C-G sequential. Phases F+G are this CLI's own logic (the other 5 are separate substrate-native CLIs already spun as beads): Phase F (bind) generates the app project — Package.swift with the generated CLI as a Swift Package dependency, project.yml via existing scaffold-display-manifest pattern + AppIcon via generate-icons, Sources/{App.swift, ContentView.swift (from reconstructed views), DataLayer.swift (wires CLI's typed API to ObservableObject state)}; Phase G (register) — SubstrateRegistryVault + SubstrateWorkspaceVault + substrate-apps-input.json + foundry visibility. Output app lands at wrkstrm-research/.../generative-ui/gym/<slug>.mac.app/ with AppKind=demoGym initial classification. Location: clia-org/.../tooling/spm/scaffold-app-from-web-cli/ likely. Composed with existing wrkstrm-identifier verbs (no new primitives at the integration layer).
resolutionNote: Closed 2026-05-17. Orchestrator shipped inline at clia-org/private/universal/domain/tooling/spm/scaffold-app-from-web-cli/ (executable product scaffold-app-from-web-cli; library ScaffoldAppFromWebKit). v1 implementation inlines Phases B (TrafficCapture via Foundation URLSession), D (CLICodegen emits Swift Package with typed Models + HTTPClient + AsyncParsableCommand subcommands), F (AppCodegen emits SwiftUI app package with project.yml + AppDisplayManifest + App.swift + ContentView.swift + DataLayer.swift), G (SubstrateRegistryVault.upsert + save). Phase A and C still stubbed for v2 (kept as dependent beads though closure here unblocks the orchestrator's existence). Phase E integration with screenshot-reconstruction-gym still pending. First satellite scaffolded + verified: hackernews → 312d7253-cli installed at ~/.swiftpm/bin/, live HN Firebase data confirmed via topstories/item/maxitem calls.
